title: Expose borrowed iteration with iter
scenario: Callers often need to inspect a collection wrapper without consuming it. A borrowed iterator exposes a view without leaking fields.

hints.body: Keep the Exposing iteration on collection wrappers code active and add OrderLines::iter(&self) -> std::slice::Iter<'_, OrderLine>.
Use the Rust API named in this lesson instead of adding a side helper; the previous arc code should stay in the main solution.
One complete solution for Expose borrowed iteration with iter is shown below.

completionExplanation: Expose borrowed iteration with iter now extends the active Exposing iteration on collection wrappers code with wrap borrowed iter. The previous lesson's behavior remains in the main solution rather than an archived module.
